HDFS.eligibility_note: Controlled direct transfer-service failure from the short rq3_test_set pool, frozen under the more conservative link-health and retransmission-control action contract.
Direct transfer-service failure case kept as one anchor exemplar for the transfer-link family, using the same conservative transfer-control action contract as the other serving-exception anchors.

Benchmark_v2 direct transfer-service failure retained as a second stable transfer anchor, but relabeled to the conservative link-check and retransmission-control action because the stricter prompt contract does not support the stronger re-replication action from the visible evidence.
Pipeline rebuild case kept on the enriched source so the frozen context exposes only generic write-pipeline evidence instead of a one-line alert.
Pipeline failure anchored on PacketResponder termination, but frozen on the enriched source so the visible context remains generic pipeline chatter rather than a receiver-specific fault.

Replaces the unstable Receiving-block anchor with a PacketResponder-side received-block case from the enriched source, preserving pipeline diversity while removing the stochastic DataXceiver ambiguity that kept flipping agent outputs.
Allocation-pressure case kept on the short rq3_test_set source to avoid multi-family contamination in HDFS context.

HDFS.alert_match: Unexpected error trying to delete block blk_-3544583377289625738. BlockInfo not found in volumeMap.
HDFS.eligibility_note: Retained as the stale-block cleanup anchor because the delete failure is explicit and historically stable.
Block-map consistency case included so the storage family does not collapse into allocate-vs-delete only.

OpenStack.alert_match: The instance sync for host 'cp-1.slowvm1.tcloud-pg0.utah.cloudlab.us' did not match. Re-created its InstanceList.
OpenStack.eligibility_note: One anchor inventory-drift case is kept on the explicit scheduler mismatch line so the family does not disappear under high paraphrase.

External-events line retained in the instance-state family, but relabeled to the power-state / rebuild action because the frozen context consistently reads as a spawning-state drift rather than a pure inventory resynchronization problem.
Power-state drift case intentionally anchored on build-latency chatter instead of sync_power_state or a lifecycle event.

OpenStack.alert_match: Took 19.47 seconds to spawn the instance on the hypervisor.
OpenStack.eligibility_note: Second power-state case keeps the selected alert weaker than sync_power_state while still support-complete in context.
Termination/restore case is kept to prevent the instance-state family from collapsing into inventory-vs-power only.
Cache-cleanup image case built around in-use bookkeeping rather than Unknown base file.
One benchmark_v2 image-cache bookkeeping case is retained so cleanup-style cache maintenance is not sourced only from the enriched slice.
Image-chain repair case frozen on the direct image-creation alert, with repair-only context filtering so the benchmark does not hinge on the noisier cache-bookkeeping surface form.
Second repair-base-image case anchored on direct image creation rather than later host-claim bookkeeping.

Hadoop.eligibility_note: Worker-isolation case kept on a direct retry-connect alert so the visible evidence matches node-isolation semantics without cleanup contamination.
Worker cleanup/reschedule anchor built around an explicit failed HDFS output cleanup line.
Second cleanup/reschedule worker case chosen from a separate incident so worker failure does not collapse to one alert instance.
RM-channel recovery anchor relabeled onto the explicit RMCommunicator :8030 retry signal instead of a worker-failure interpretation.

Hadoop.alert_match: forcibly closed by the remote host
Hadoop.eligibility_note: Worker-side RPC/channel disruption case anchored on forced-close transport failure.

Hadoop.alert_match: forcibly closed by the remote host
Hadoop.eligibility_note: Second worker-RPC recovery case kept on another forced-close socket failure to stabilize the control-worker slice.
Shuffle-pressure anchor built on an explicit spill-to-disk signal, not on cleanup fallout.
Second shuffle-pressure case chosen from the same storage-heavy pool but with a distinct spill instance.
Third shuffle-pressure case retained so the storage slice is a clean spill-pressure subpool rather than a mixed cleanup bucket.
